title: Gambling, Games & the Midnight Guillotine Quiz

questions.question: Which game carries a specific 'cursed' -250 outcome as one of its possible results?

answers.text: /gamble
answers.feedback: /gamble is the simple once-a-day game; the -250 trapdoor is specific to /scratch.

answers.text: /scratch
answers.feedback: Correct. The scratch card prize table is 0/+10/+25/+50/+100/+250/+500, plus one cursed -250 trapdoor outcome.

answers.text: /holy-lottery
answers.feedback: The Holy Lottery pays fixed prizes to places 1-5; it doesn't have a cursed negative outcome baked into its structure.

answers.text: /horse-race
answers.feedback: Horse racing pays out based on displayed odds on your bet, not a fixed prize table with a cursed trapdoor result.

questions.question: A member answers a /trivia question first, expecting to win, but instead gets fined 500 credits. What most likely happened?

answers.text: They triggered the game's 10% 'boobytrap' chance instead of winning
answers.feedback: Correct. /trivia has a built-in 10% chance that the first correct answer triggers a 500-credit fine instead of a win.

answers.text: They answered too slowly and were fined for taking too long
answers.feedback: Speed isn't penalised directly — the boobytrap chance is what can turn a correct, fast answer into a fine.

answers.text: Someone else appealed their win to Karen and had it reversed
answers.feedback: Appeals apply to a player's own losses, not to reversing someone else's trivia win.

answers.text: The guillotine happened to fire at the same moment
answers.feedback: The guillotine runs on its own nightly schedule at NZ midnight — it isn't tied to individual trivia rounds.

questions.question: Which of these are true about the Midnight Narkcoin Guillotine? (Select all that apply)

answers.text: It runs automatically every night at NZ midnight, without requiring anyone to trigger it
answers.feedback: Correct. It's a fully automatic, recurring event, not something a member or admin has to invoke.

answers.text: It only affects the top three positive Nark Credit holders
answers.feedback: Correct. Only the top three positive balances are clipped — everyone else is untouched by this specific mechanic.

answers.text: It permanently bans the top holders from gambling for 24 hours
answers.feedback: The guillotine clips 50% of the top three balances — it doesn't impose any gambling ban.

answers.text: It exists to prevent any single member's balance from growing without limit
answers.feedback: Correct. It's the mechanism that keeps the leaderboard competitive by capping runaway accumulation at the top.

questions.question: What is the significance of the Watercooler rule that members with more than 10 Nark Credits are immune from bot warnings and bans?

answers.text: It's a permanent, unconditional exemption from all moderation everywhere in the community
answers.feedback: It's narrower than that — it's Watercooler-only, and normal moderation still applies below the 10-credit threshold and in every other channel.

answers.text: It's a satirical jab at how real wealth buys protection, deliberately undercut by the fact the guillotine prevents anyone staying rich for long
answers.feedback: Correct. The joke only lands because the 'wealth' that buys protection is also the exact thing the nightly guillotine keeps knocking back down.

answers.text: It only applies to Clawd's own wallet, not to community members
answers.feedback: The immunity applies to community members holding more than 10 credits in Watercooler, not specifically to the bot's own balance.

answers.text: It replaces the need for the Midnight Guillotine entirely
answers.feedback: The two mechanics coexist — the guillotine still runs nightly regardless of who currently qualifies for the immunity threshold.

questions.question: Match each game or mechanic to its defining feature:

answers.text: /horse-race
answers.matchText: 60-second betting window on 8 deterministic horses, live commentary every 5 seconds

answers.text: /higher-lower
answers.matchText: Woolworths price-guessing game with an asymmetric win/loss payout

answers.text: narkstreetbets
answers.matchText: Full paper-trading stock market simulator with margin and shorting

answers.text: Midnight Narkcoin Guillotine
answers.matchText: Nightly automatic clip of 50% from the top three positive balances
